REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS XELAN PROP 1, LLC AND ANNA KIHAGI'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, NEGLIGENCE, IIED, NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION AND WRONGFUL EVICTION. ALL OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ON THE ALLEGED VIOLATION OF THE ORDINANCE AND ARE SUBJECT TO 1-YEAR SOL. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS TO AMEND TO ALLEGE: (1) FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, SUPPORTING TOLLING OR DELAYED DISCOVERY; OR (2) RE-ALLEGE THESE CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON FACTS NOT ARISING OUT OF VIOLATION OF SFRO. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION, IN PART, ALSO ARISES OUT OF EVENTS OCCURRING DURING PLAINTIFF'S TENANCY (INDEPENDENT FROM THE OMI). PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AND ALLEGE FACTS (NOT CONCLUSIONS) SUPPORTING TOLLING OR DELAYED DISCOVERY. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF WARRANTY OF QUIET ENJOYMENT. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS IN PART BASED ON EVENTS OCCURRING "DURING THE COURT OF PLAINTIFF'S TENANCY" (INDEPENDENT FROM THE OMI) AND THEREFORE IS NOT BARRED BY THE 1-YEAR SOL. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tyson Redenbarger, Esq. 415-318-5709 of Hooshmand Law Group for Plaintiff KELLY KIMBALL.
Karen Uchiyama, Esq. 415-563-9300 for Defendant Anne Kihagi.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF KELLY KIMBALL's Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling in it's entirety. Plaintiff Kelly Kimball's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is denied as to Mr. Kimball's proposed eighth cause of action for breach of contract and granted as to Mr. Kimball's proposed ninth cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law, Business and Professions Code 17200 et seq. The proposed breach of contract claim is a damages claim premised on an alleged violation of the owner move-in provisions of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance and thus is time-barred per CCP 340(1) as reflected in the court's ruling on the most recent motion for judgment on the pleadings filed by defendants Anna Kihagi and Xelan Prop 1, LLC. (Menefee v. Ostwari (1991) 228 Cal. App. 3d 239, 245). The allegations in the proposed breach of contract claim regarding the CCSF v. Kihagi case do not avoid the statute of limitations of section 340(1) or provide for a new or different basis for Mr. Kimball to seek damages for the alleged illegal owner move-in by defendants. In contrast to the proposed breach of contract claim, the proposed UCL claim is not a damages claim and thus is not governed by CCP 340(1) and instead is governed by the four year limitations periods applicable to all UCL claims. Menefee is not applicable to the UCL claim because, by its express terms, that decision only applies to damages claims. A prevailing plaintiff on a UCL claim "may not receive damages, much less treble damages "and is "generally limited to injunctive relief and restitution." (Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. (1999) 20 Cal. 4th 163, 179).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Off Calendar Apr-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Tyson Redenbarger, Esq. of the Hooshmand Law Group, T:415.318.5709, for plaintiff/moving party;
Karen Uchiyama, Esq., for defendant/opposing party [no card].

RULING: The tentative ruling was not contested but defendant's counsel objected to the proposed order. After conferring, the parties agreed on the additional language in the proposed order. Thefore, there being no other objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling:

PLAINTIFF KELLY KIMBALL's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Xelan Prop 1, LLC As A Named Defendant is GRANTED. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy allowing amendments to pleadings and defendants have not identified any prejudice to them if the motion is granted. The argument that Xelan cannot be liable for any of the claims alleged by Mr. Kimball is best addressed on a demurrer or other pleading motion, not a motion to amend, although it appears that there is no legal impediment to imposing liability on Xelan merely because it is an entity rather than an individual.

Off Calendar Jan-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq., T:415.563.9301, for defendants/moving party;
Mark Hooshmand, Esq. with Tyson Redenbarger, Esq., of the Hooshmand Law Group, T:415.318.5709, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS XELAN PROP 1 AND ANN KIHAGI'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, on the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Kelly Kimball is OFF CALENDAR in part, DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The motion is OFF CALENDAR as to Xelan, DENIED as to the third cause of action for breach of warranty of quiet enjoyment and GRANTED without leave to amend as to all other causes of action. Mr. Kimball dismissed Xelan on January 5, 2018. The November 30, 2017 order on the prior motion for judgment on the pleadings was denied as to the quiet enjoyment claim and thus Ms. Kihagi may not make another motion for judgment on the pleadings on that claim absent compliance with CCP 1008, which she has not shown. The November 30 order gave leave to amend to Mr. Kimball to allege facts (as opposed to conclusions) supporting tolling or delayed discovery and/or to allege facts not arising from a violation of the owner move-in provisions of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance. Mr. Kimball has not alleged such facts. The first amended complaint does not contain any facts supporting either tolling or delayed discovery nor do any of the claims other than the quiet enjoyment claim allege any facts not based on an owner move-in violation. The Court also grants plaintiff leave to file a second amended complaint. Counsel for Ms. Kihagi submitted a proposed order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

[Order signed after hearing.]

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Karen Y. Uchiyama, esq. (415-563-9300) for Defendants/Moving party XELAN PROP 1, LLC and ANNA KIHAGI.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs XELAN PROP 1, LLC and ANNA KIHAGI's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to causes of action for declaratory relief, negligence, IIED, negligent misrepresentation, intentional misrepresentation and wrongful eviction. All of these causes of action are based on the alleged violation of the ordinance and are subject to 1-year SOL. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to amend to allege: (1) facts, not conclusions, supporting tolling or delayed discovery; OR (2) re-allege these causes of action based on facts not arising out of violation of SFRO. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend as to Negligence cause of action. This cause of action, in part, also arises out of events occurring during Plaintiff's tenancy (independent from the OMI). Plaintiff shall have 10 days to amend the complaint and allege facts (not conclusions) supporting tolling or delayed discovery. Motion is DENIED as to the cause of action for Breach of Warranty of Quiet Enjoyment. This cause of action is in part based on events occurring "during the court of Plaintiff's tenancy" (independent from the OMI) and therefore is not barred by the 1-year SOL. Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
